{"key_event":"World War II colonial economic mobilization","reasoning":"Wartime mining, taxation, forced production, labor controls, militarization, and strategic uranium sharply increased the colonial state’s extractive and coercive reach, pushing it toward the upper end of strong state capacity. Civil society remained extremely weak because strikes and mutiny showed embryonic collective capacity but were violently suppressed and outweighed by intensified surveillance, forced labor, and state-company control.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":1.2,"state_change":0.7,"state_power":7.9}
